state.activeDirective: Pack 4B visual benchmark: three worker-housing courtyard families.

benchmark.purpose: Deterministic Pack 4B courtyard composition, entrance approach, winter surface, lighting, LOD, depth, quality, and fallback review.
benchmark.productionContract: The benchmark object is development metadata ignored by SaveLoad. This remains a normal version 4 save and adds no production UI or save fields.

environmentPlanner.contract: Environment compositions are derived from immutable owner identity and map seed. They are never serialized into the save.

northWestServiceEdgeContract.contract: The checked-in development save must produce this north-west service-edge composition through the production planner.

winterSurfaceContract.statement: Static seasonal ground, dirt, forest, roof snow, and cliff lips are reviewed without nondeterministic live particles.

fallbackContract.boundary: These cases prove graceful local recovery for the two authored Pack 4 atlases and one selected environment frame. They do not exhaust every frame, decode, or transport corruption mode.

cases.atlasFailure: remove service-shelter-nw-winter-near from environment-worker-housing-1x.json before navigation

reviewContract: Pair each family day/night at identical near camera settings.
Inspect each family at authored far LOD and High quality.
Inspect the far Low-quality overview for graceful degradation.
Inspect the elevated terrace and cliff boundary in both overview frames.
Inspect static winter roof/ground/path separation with weather particles disabled.
Inspect the deterministic north-west service edge through the runtime fixture.
Treat the mixed-family overview as foundation evidence only, not full Pack 4 completion evidence.
Inspect building and environment procedural fallbacks after aborting each authored atlas frames request.
Inspect one selected environment-frame loss with unaffected authored parts still present.

claimBoundaries: Dirt paths prove visible same-elevation approach coherence, not pedestrian pathfinding or door routing.
The version 4 footprint model does not rotate buildings; this benchmark makes no orientation or inward-facing-facade claim.
A fixed save and clock do not make screenshots byte-identical across browsers or machines.
Snapshot light state does not by itself prove visible day/night contrast; paired visual review is required.
Static validation cannot prove snow separation or depth composition; the approved capture matrix remains required evidence.
The mixed-family overview is foundation evidence for shared composition and depth only; it is not evidence that every Pack 4 family is implemented.
The fallback captures cover whole building-atlas loss, whole environment-atlas loss, and one selected environment-frame loss; they do not exhaust every frame or corruption mode.
